title: Do plants get tanged was Being tanged and getting better
display_title: Do plants get tanged was Being tanged and getting better
wikitext:
[]: Do plants get tanged? (was: Being tanged and getting better) Post by TehDonutKing » Mon Apr 26, 2010 4:34 pm Allemann wrote: Pen Pen was left behind.

[]: Post by NatTheWriter » Mon Apr 26, 2010 4:42 pm TehDonutKing wrote: But ALL creatures on earth were born from Lilith. Pen Pen was tanged offscreen.

[]: Post by Xard » Mon Apr 26, 2010 4:56 pm Presumably they know the art of auto-detanging since we see trees and shit when Rei starts to break down (literally, hurrr)

[]: Post by Seele00TextOnly » Mon Apr 26, 2010 5:54 pm This will probably end up needing a split, maybe to an existing thread that addresses it. Plants would not become tanged along with the rest of life because they do not have souls; the Third Impact involves an anti-A.T. field that breaches all and brings the souls within together. The A.T field is the boundary of an ego that allows for separate individuals. Seeing as plants have neither minds or egos and almost certainly not souls (though I suspect Shinto may disagree with this point), it stands to reason that none of them joined the orange orgy in the sky. I also questioned whether animals get tanged until I remembered that Pen^2 shows up in EoTV to squawk out his congratulations. I suppose that's all the evidence needed, but am curious what others think of it.

[]: Post by NemZ » Mon Apr 26, 2010 6:05 pm Yes... but the landscape at the end certainly looks to have been blasted of all life, plants included. I don't see how "plants don't have souls" can be accepted simply as conjecture since there doesn't seem to be any particular reason why they would be denied, being just another variety of lilith-created life.

[]: Post by Seele00TextOnly » Mon Apr 26, 2010 6:16 pm Well it is demonstrated that the dead join the LCL sea as well (unless it's just the recently dead, in which case I call shenanigans). If plantlife were also collected, those same dead-ish trees/planks like the one Shinji nailed the cross to would have been absorbed. If they were to have rematerialized of their own will they wouldn't have come back as dead planks. Also, think of all the clothes (not this again!) that we see left behind after the tanging. Are none of those made from cotton and other plant fibers? Would not they have joined in the festivities? On another note, anything that rematerializes does so within the sea of LCL and has to return to the shore. Did the trees wash up on the beach and Shinji dig to plant them, roots and all? Unless I'm getting many more things wrong here, which is known to happen.

[]: Post by Legendary » Mon Apr 26, 2010 6:18 pm Plants were almost certainly tanged with everything else. They have a lot more going on for them than we humans give them credit for, since their entire structure is quite alien to us, and thus thematically it makes no sense for them to be denied. They also popped back into existence as quickly as possible, as Xard pointed out. The devastation at the beach is probably more related to the N2 warhead that was dropped on Tokyo-3.

[]: Post by Mr. Tines » Mon Apr 26, 2010 6:20 pm Legendary wrote: The devastation at the beach is probably more related to the N2 warhead that was dropped on Tokyo-3.

[]: Post by TehDonutKing » Mon Apr 26, 2010 6:21 pm The plants aren't sentient, as far as I can tell, so they wouldn't have souls, so no tanging.

[]: Post by Ironfoot » Mon Apr 26, 2010 6:32 pm I don't think that one can say that ALL plants on Earth were completely killed off just from one shot of devastation in Japan. That's a very large assumption. It makes sense that the area is devoid of life, its the ground zero of Third Impact. Several explosions occurred there, including the N2 mine, Shinji's Anti-AT Field explosion as well as the rising of the Black Moon. I would be surprised if the area wasn't devastated. But to say that the exclusion of plant life in the area serves as the evidence of the extinction of all plant life on Earth is a bit silly. In reality, there's simply not enough evidence to tell. There's also not enough information to determine if plants would have souls or not. The only thing worthy of conjecture would be to borrow supporting beliefs from one of the religious influences of Evangelion, if any, to support such a notion. All else is fanwank. IMO, I highly doubt it.

[]: Post by Legendary » Mon Apr 26, 2010 6:51 pm TehDonutKing wrote: The plants aren't sentient, as far as I can tell, so they wouldn't have souls, so no tanging.

[]: Post by Seele00TextOnly » Mon Apr 26, 2010 7:06 pm The burden of proof of whether things have to spawn in the LCL sea or not doesn't flow in that direction. The spirits/bodies became the LCL, therefore they must come directly from it. It has to fight for survival from there forward, including making it to the shore. It may not be fair, but neither is killing everything. Rei isn't omnipotent. She doesn't get to say 'well, for the convenience of my human children that may or may not return, I allow my plant children to come back safe and cozy in the ground where they belong'. She doesn't even get to choose if anything comes back at all; it must happen of that lifeforms' own free will. The 'plants get tanged and can come back' theory is founded on far more baseless assumptions than the opposing view.

[]: Post by NemZ » Mon Apr 26, 2010 7:06 pm I made no claims about plant life around the world... just that there isn't any near Shinji, which still has interesting implications regarding what, if anything, he's been eating. I don't think being sentient or not has anything to do with it... life = soul, at least in NGE. In real life, on the other hand... nah, not today The question of dead trees is an interesting one, however, implying that it is indeed only the recently dead that need apply for instrumentality. I think discussion was had previously in another thread about the possibility of a soul degrading over time as the body it is attached to breaks down. Otherwise tang would be the natural result of any death, making eating a very tricky proposition.

[]: Post by Sailor Star Dust » Mon Apr 26, 2010 7:18 pm It's probably been said already, but we see trees and such when GNR's head falls to Earth (and during the imagery of life returning when Shinji has a final talk with Adam and Lilith, the images progress from red soil = trees = people = a city and last people walking through the city). Thus, either plants and "lower" life forms (animals instead of the human animal) don't need to consciously think about living the same way humans do, or they just automatically return to life so to speak. I think in this case, it'd make sense that humanity would be the last form of life to return from the LCL Sea. (Granted, we see no animals wandering around, I'm just basing this information off of what we do see with plants.) /My own two cents.

[]: Post by Legendary » Mon Apr 26, 2010 7:27 pm Okay, let's analyze the following facts. 1) During the tanging scene, we see crosses springing up ALL OVER THE PLACE. We never see one hint of plant life during this sequence. 2) Rei says "The primordial soup of life," and "everything has just been joined into one." These two statements are amazingly broad if only humans or only animals were lumped into the LCL Sea. 3) When Rei "dies", we see plants again for the first time since the end of the world began. They're not in the sea. They're safe and sound where they belong. You also say that Rei isn't omnipotent. I'm sorry, but I have immense difficulty believing that a being with the power to move through time and appear in billions of locations at once can't arrange for sessile living things to respawn where they should. In much the same way, for all we really know, Shinji and Asuka only spawned in the sea because they live by a body of water. Perhaps people who normally live in the Sahara respawn there. We don't know how the physics of souls work. Not in the least. But from what we see in the movie, we have reasonable indications that plants get tanged and that they're later okay. You don't have to believe this. But the movie seems to lean towards the "everything died; plants came back okay" interpretation.

[]: Post by ran1 » Mon Apr 26, 2010 8:00 pm My issue with plants being tanged is thus: -considering that many of our foodstuffs are made with plant materials, would the local candy bar at the local Tokyo-3 Kwik-e-Mart be tanged along with the 100 year oak tree? Would all the food be gone? Considering that the dead are consumed, this means that the local butcher shop would be devoid of meat as well, which would render all possible food sources gone with the wind. -this would pose major problems for Shinji's survival following his return from the LCL. How long can a traumatized 14 year old last without food? That being said, I have no fundamental issue with all plant-life and by products of plant life being consumed so long as we realize that Shinji probably hasn't been sitting on that beach as long as people like to speculate here. It also supports the "ending is nihilistic" viewpoint that some espouse.

[]: Post by NemZ » Mon Apr 26, 2010 8:53 pm ran1 wrote: -this would pose major problems for Shinji's survival following his return from the LCL. How long can a traumatized 14 year old last without food?

[]: Post by Legendary » Mon Apr 26, 2010 9:00 pm Except post-Instrumentality clearly shows some living plants and some dead ones, ran1, so there isn't a lack of food.

[]: Post by Seele00TextOnly » Mon Apr 26, 2010 9:52 pm To sum up what I'm thinking now about all of this, the only question that remains is whether the plants get tanged or not. As stated they are obviously there after 3I, either through remaining because they weren't tanged or because they returned in some manner as proposed. I'm not thinking this question can be answered with the data we have available. I am less inclined to see plants as having souls and am unwilling to fanwank that they could have re-materializing safe and snug embedded in the ground, so I propose in the wake of contrary evidence that they were never tanged at all. ...but that's just stating the obvious, over and over...

[]: Post by Legendary » Mon Apr 26, 2010 9:55 pm Except you haven't addressed the criticism of this theory in the form of the complete lack of ANYTHING living during the tanging scene and some explanatory dialogue. That's why I say plants were tanged, and I fanwank not because I think it's cooler, but because the dialogue and scenery force me to.
summary:

[]: Post by NatTheWriter » Mon Apr 26, 2010 4:42 pm TehDonutKing wrote: But ALL creatures on earth were born from Lilith. Pen Pen was tanged offscreen.
